items.user_turn: Okay, can you give me a simple, low-effort plan to get useful value from my existing frequent‑flyer balances for one upcoming trip? For background: I can share balances and account IDs so you can judge realism — American AAdvantage: 85,400 miles (A12345678); United MileagePlus: 60,200 miles (U76543210); Flying Blue (Air France/KLM): 22,500 miles (FB998877). My travel window is June 12–18, 2026 and I must depart the morning of June 12 (return within one day). My total round-trip budget ceiling is $1,200 and I’m willing to pay up to $300 extra to avoid complicated award routings — I don’t want to spend hours gaming blackout dates or micro-optimizing; I just want clear options (use miles vs pay cash, which accounts to prioritize, and concrete booking options/tools).
items.task_goal: Give a simple, low-effort plan to get useful value from my existing frequent‑flyer balances (recommend whether to use miles or pay cash, which accounts/alliance to prioritize, and concrete booking options or tools) for an upcoming trip without requiring me to chase blackout-date gaming or micro-optimizations.
